name: azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter-py
description: Low-level exporter for sending OpenTelemetry traces, metrics, and logs to Application Insights. Use for custom OpenTelemetry pipeline configuration.
capabilities: Configure AzureMonitorTraceExporter for sending traces to Application Insights
Set up AzureMonitorMetricExporter for custom metrics collection
Implement AzureMonitorLogExporter for Python logging integration
Use Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential
Configure Application Insights sampling for production workloads
Enable offline storage for reliable telemetry export
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing only a SKILL.md file with usage examples for the azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter Python package. No executable code was scanned. Static analysis found 0 files, 0 lines of code, and 0 security issues. The skill provides legitimate Azure monitoring documentation with no malicious patterns.
valueStatement: This skill provides Claude Code with knowledge about the Azure Monitor OpenTelemetry Exporter for Python, enabling developers to send custom telemetry pipelines directly to Application Insights for monitoring and analysis.
userTitle: Export OpenTelemetry Data to Azure Application Insights
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and Application Insights resource
Does not include auto-instrumentation features (use distro package for that)
Connection string or credential must be provided externally

useCases.targetUser: DevOps engineers requiring fine-grained control over telemetry
useCases.title: Custom OpenTelemetry Pipeline
useCases.description: Build a custom telemetry pipeline with specific processors, samplers, and exporters for advanced monitoring scenarios

useCases.title: Azure-Native Monitoring
useCases.description: Integrate Python applications with Azure Application Insights for enterprise monitoring and alerting

useCases.targetUser: Backend developers working with microservice architectures
useCases.title: Multi-Service Tracing
useCases.description: Implement consistent distributed tracing across microservices with Application Insights sampling

promptTemplates.title: Basic Trace Export
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I set up AzureMonitorTraceExporter to send OpenTelemetry traces to Application Insights?
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with basic trace export

promptTemplates.title: Custom Metrics Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to configure AzureMonitorMetricExporter with a custom meter provider and export interval.

promptTemplates.title: Logging Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I integrate Python logging with AzureMonitorLogExporter using BatchLogRecordProcessor?

promptTemplates.title: AAD Authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure AzureMonitorTraceExporter to use Azure AD authentication with DefaultAzureCredential instead of instrumentation keys.

bestPractices: Use BatchSpanProcessor instead of SimpleSpanProcessor in production for better performance
Enable ApplicationInsightsSampler for consistent sampling across distributed services
Use Azure AD authentication (DefaultAzureCredential) instead of instrumentation keys for better security
antiPatterns: Do not use SimpleSpanProcessor in production as it blocks on each export
Avoid hardcoding instrumentation keys in source code - use environment variables
Do not disable offline storage without understanding the impact on telemetry reliability

faq.question: What is the difference between azure-monitor-opentelemetry and azure-monitor-opentelemetry-exporter?
faq.answer: The distro package (azure-monitor-opentelemetry) provides auto-instrumentation and ready-to-use defaults. The exporter package gives you low-level control over the OpenTelemetry pipeline.

faq.question: How do I authenticate without instrumentation keys?
faq.answer: Use Azure AD authentication by passing a credential like DefaultAzureCredential to the exporter constructor instead of a connection string.

faq.question: Can I use this with sovereign clouds?
faq.answer: Yes, configure the credential with the appropriate authority (AzureGovernment or AzureChina) and use the sovereign cloud connection string.

faq.question: What happens if Application Insights is unavailable?
faq.answer: With offline storage enabled (default), telemetry is stored locally and retried when connection is restored. Disable offline storage if you don't need this reliability.

faq.question: How do I sample traces consistently?
faq.answer: Use ApplicationInsightsSampler which coordinates sampling decisions across service calls to ensure complete traces.

faq.question: Does this support all telemetry types?
faq.answer: Yes, there are separate exporters for traces (AzureMonitorTraceExporter), metrics (AzureMonitorMetricExporter), and logs (AzureMonitorLogExporter).
